axes.name: Database / persistence
axes.why_needed: Persistent relational data must be stored
evidence_quotes: 1. **Persistent data** — workspaces, users, tasks, comments, assignments must be stored relationally and survive restarts.
7. **File attachments** — users attach files (up to ~25 MB) to tasks; raw files stored outside the database.

axes.name: File storage
axes.why_needed: Raw files stored outside the database
evidence_quotes: 7. **File attachments** — users attach files (up to ~25 MB) to tasks; raw files stored outside the database.

axes.name: Authentication / access
evidence_quotes: 2. **User authentication** — each user logs in; workspace-level data isolation required.
- Future SaaS phase may add billing and multi-tenant dashboards — choose a stack that does not require a full rewrite later.

axes.name: HTTP API layer
axes.why_needed: Client talks to backend over an API
evidence_quotes: 3. **HTTP API** — the web client talks to the backend over an API for every action (create task, assign, comment).

axes.name: Web frontend
evidence_quotes: 3. **HTTP API** — the web client talks to the backend over an API for every action (create task, assign, comment).
4. **Web frontend** — a dashboard where users see their tasks and workspace activity.

axes.name: Hosting / deployment
evidence_quotes: - Budget-sensitive: prefer managed services, keep monthly cost predictable.

axes.name: Background jobs / scheduling
evidence_quotes: 6. **Background jobs** — a daily digest email summarising open tasks per user, sent on a schedule.

axes.name: Email channel
evidence_quotes: 5. **Email notifications** — when a task is assigned or commented on, the assignee receives an email.
6. **Background jobs** — a daily digest email summarising open tasks per user, sent on a schedule.

axes.name: LLM / AI reasoning
evidence_quotes: - Which LLM (if any) for the "suggest task priority" feature is NEEDS CLARIFICATION.
